response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates exceptional full-stack engineering capability and strong software craftsmanship practices, they fall significantly short on core minimum requirements. With less than 2 years of hands-on C++ experience against a stated 8-year requirement, and no demonstrated technical leadership in cloud or compute-focused roles, they lack the specialized depth this technical leadership position demands. Their career trajectory emphasizes breadth over the focused infrastructure expertise this role requires.
strengths: Exceptional full-stack versatility and language agility—successfully adopted C++, D, Java, TypeScript, and others, demonstrating strong learning capacity for new technical domains
Deep software craftsmanship culture—TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and organized refactoring/testing initiatives reflect commitment to engineering excellence and establishing best practices
Proven large-scale infrastructure experience—delivered systems handling millions of requests/second with Kubernetes, Docker, and AWS CDK; real-time distributed systems work at Dunnhumby
concerns: Critical C++ programming gap—only ~2 years of documented C++ experience (National Instruments, thesis project) versus the 8-year minimum requirement, representing a fundamental mismatch

No explicit technical leadership track record—all roles are individual contributor or staff engineer titles without clear evidence of team leadership, mentoring responsibilities, or DevOps team management

Career focused on generalist full-stack development rather than specialized compute platform, cloud architecture, or infrastructure-domain expertise; limited direct exposure to the core technical domain
